functional_requirements.title: Task Creation
functional_requirements.description: Users can create a new task with a title and optional description. Each task gets a unique auto-incremented ID, a status of 'pending', and a creation timestamp.

functional_requirements.title: Task Listing
functional_requirements.description: Users can list all tasks, optionally filtered by status (pending/completed/all). Output shows task ID, title, status, and creation time.

functional_requirements.title: Task Completion
functional_requirements.description: Users can mark a task as completed by its ID. Validates that the task exists and returns a success or error message.

functional_requirements.title: Task Deletion
functional_requirements.description: Users can delete a task by its ID. Validates that the task exists and returns a success or error message.

non_functional_requirements.description: The system should provide clear success and error messages for all operations.
The system should handle invalid inputs gracefully without crashing.

acceptance_criteria.description: Creating a task with a title and optional description results in a new task with a unique ID, status 'pending', and a timestamp.
Listing tasks without filter returns all tasks; filtering by 'pending' or 'completed' returns only matching tasks.
Completing an existing task changes its status to 'completed' and returns a success message. Completing a non-existent task returns an error.
Deleting an existing task removes it from the list and returns a success message. Deleting a non-existent task returns an error.

storage.details: No database persistence; all tasks are stored in memory during the session.

glossary.Task: A unit of work with a title, optional description, unique ID, status (pending/completed), and creation timestamp.
glossary.Status: The current state of a task, either 'pending' or 'completed'.
glossary.Auto-incremented ID: A unique integer identifier assigned sequentially to each new task.

global_state_sources.description: In-memory list of all tasks created during the session.
Counter for generating unique task IDs.

input_spec.description: The system receives input as a JSON object with a 'command' string and optional 'task_data' dict.

schema.task_data: dict (optional, contains title, description, task_id, status_filter)

output_spec.description: The system produces output as a JSON object with a 'success' boolean, 'message' string, and optional 'data' dict.

schema.data: dict (optional, contains task_id, tasks list, etc.)
